name: Eswatini

colors.symbolism: the mineral resources of the country
the colours of the Nguni shield placed at the centre
the hide of the Nguni shield and the spear-shafts

description: Five horizontal stripes of blue, yellow, red, yellow, and blue (the central red being broadest, the yellow stripes narrowest). Centred over the red stripe is a large Nguni ox-hide shield in black and white, laid horizontally across two crossed spears and a staff with tassels of widow-bird and lourie feathers.
history: Adopted on 6 October 1968 following independence from the United Kingdom on 6 September 1968. The design originates in a military banner given by King Sobhuza II to the Swazi Pioneer Corps in 1941. The country was renamed from Swaziland to Eswatini in 2018, but the flag was retained.

features.symbolism: the central red band represents past battles; the yellow stripes the mineral wealth of the country; the blue stripes peace and stability.
a black-and-white Nguni ox-hide shield laid horizontally over two spears and a fighting staff (umgobho) decorated with feather tassels (injobo); represents protection of the kingdom.
